program_outcomes.name: Curriculum Creation, Evaluation, and Implementation
program_outcomes.description: The learner evaluates and implements curriculum that aligns with standards, instructional goals, and student needs to support effective teaching and learning across educational settings.

program_outcomes.name: Assessment for Learning and Student Learning Data
program_outcomes.description: The learner applies valid and reliable assessment practices and data analysis to improve instruction, support student achievement, and communicate assessment data to teachers, school leaders, district administrators, stakeholders, and parents.

program_outcomes.name: Evidence-Based Instructional Practices
program_outcomes.description: The learner applies valid and reliable assessment practices and data analysis to improve instruction, support student achievement, and communicate assessment data to teachers, school leaders, district administrators, stakeholders, and parents.

program_outcomes.name: Cultural Competence
program_outcomes.description: The learner applies valid and reliable assessment practices and data analysis to improve instruction, support student achievement, and communicate assessment data to teachers, school leaders, district administrators, stakeholders, and parents.

program_outcomes.name: Instructional Leadership and Collaboration
program_outcomes.description: The learner applies principles of instructional leadership and collaboration with educators, stakeholders, and instructional teams to design and implement curriculum, assessment, and instruction to improve student achievement.

program_outcomes.name: Foundations of Educational Research
program_outcomes.description: The learner applies educational research and data analysis methods to evaluate curriculum, assessment, and instruction.

program_outcomes.name: Educational Technology Tools and Principles
program_outcomes.description: The learner selects, evaluates, and integrates educational technology tools and emerging digital resources to enhance engagement, accessibility, assessment, and instruction for all learners.

courses.name: Human Development and Learning Science
courses.display_name: EXXX: Human Development and Learning Science
courses.scope_notes: This course gives a survey-level focus on how educators create supportive learning environments by modeling skills through intentional design and evaluation. It emphasizes the application of evidence-based strategies to motivate students, including careful vetting of instructional technologies. This course also explores developmental strategies that support students’ social-emotional growth, while connecting theories of learning and human development to modern classroom practice, including key principles from the Science of Reading.

competencies.title: Explains Foundational Educational Theories
competencies.statement: The learner explains foundational educational theories and their historical significance in the classroom.

skills.title: Educational Theory Impact Description
skills.statement: Describe how educational theory impacts student learning.

skills.title: Learning Experience Design
skills.statement: Apply learning theories to the design of learning experiences.

competencies.title: Examines Foundational Educational Theories
competencies.statement: The learner examines foundational educational theories in respect to learner growth and development.

skills.title: Evidence-Based Instructional Practice Analysis
skills.statement: Analyze evidence-based instructional practices.

skills.title: Solution Reexamination
skills.statement: Reexamine a problem and its solution for improvement opportunities.

competencies.title: Applies Human Development and Learning Science
competencies.statement: The learner applies human development and learning science to educational practices.

skills.title: Create Instruction that Encourages Construction of Knowledge
skills.statement: Create instruction that encourages learners' construction of knowledge, skill acquisition, and disciplined thinking processes.

skills.title: Instructional Strategy Implementation
skills.statement: Implement the use of  instructional strategies that aid in improving student retention.

competencies.title: Evaluates Theories of Understanding and Knowledge Retention
competencies.statement: The learner evaluates theories of understanding and knowledge retention in the contemporary classroom.

skills.title: Knowledge Retention Improvement
skills.statement: Improve knowledge retention through the use of complementary text and graphics in instruction.

skills.title: Higher-Order and Metacognitive Instructional Strategies
skills.statement: Select instructional strategies that develop higher-order questioning skills and metacognitive processes.

skills.title: Select Strategies to Develop Higher Order Thinking
skills.statement: Select instructional strategies that engage learners in developing higher-order thinking skills.

courses.name: Collaboration and Consultation in Education
courses.display_name: EXXX: Collaboration and Consultation in Education
courses.scope_notes: This course focuses on instructional leadership with an emphasis on curriculum development and implementation across educational settings. It explores the application of leadership strategies to support effective instruction through collaboration with teacher groups, departments, school boards, families, communities, and other stakeholders. The course also dives into the use of data-informed decision-making for curriculum alignment, professional development planning, and teaching practices and instruction.

competencies.title: Differentiates between Communication, Collaboration, and Consultation
competencies.statement: The learner differentiates between communication, collaboration, and consultation in learning environments.

skills.title: Analyze The Effectiveness Of Interpersonal Communication
skills.statement: Analyze the effectiveness of interpersonal communication in various contexts.

skills.title: Apply Principles Interpersonal Communication
skills.statement: Apply principles of effective interpersonal communication.

skills.title: Build Positive Culture
skills.statement: Builds a collaborative, inclusive, and positive team culture.

competencies.title: Fosters Collaborative Partnerships
competencies.statement: The learner collaborates with educators, families, and community stakeholders to build productive partnerships that support student learning, promote equity, and address diverse learning needs.

skills.title: Collaborative Environment Creation
skills.statement: Create a collaborative work environment through interpersonal communication skills.

skills.title: Team Inclusion and Collaboration Cultivation
skills.statement: Cultivate an environment of inclusion and collaboration that values the input of all team members.

competencies.title: Facilitates Professional Collaboration
competencies.statement: The learner facilitates collaboration among colleagues by leading team-based efforts, such as professional learning communities and instructional planning, to address shared goals and improve teaching and learning outcomes.

skills.title: Apply Appropriate Levels of Moderation and Community Management
skills.statement: Apply appropriate levels of moderation and community management to ensure a safe and welcoming learning community.

skills.title: Collaborative Strategy Development
skills.statement: Develop strategies with organizational team leads.

competencies.title: Leads Professional Learning through Consultation
competencies.statement: The learner applies consultation strategies to design and lead professional learning opportunities that address identified instructional needs and support continuous improvement in learning environments.

skills.title: Supervision Strategies Application
skills.statement: Apply supervision strategies and consultation practices in a given situation.

skills.title: Supervision to Improve Effectiveness Analysis
skills.statement: Analyze the uses of supervision and consultation to improve effectiveness.

courses.name: Professional and Ethical Use of Educational Technology
courses.display_name: EXXX: Professional and Ethical Use of Educational Technology
courses.scope_notes: This course focuses on the integration of educational technology across curriculum, assessment, and instruction to enhance student learning. The course explains the process of evaluating and recommending appropriate technologies, including assistive and accessibility tools, to support diverse learner needs. The course also touches on analyzing the technical aspects, ethical use, potential risks, and mitigation strategies associated with emerging technologies, including AI, to ensure responsible and effective implementation in educational settings.

competencies.title: Evaluates Educational Technology and Compliance
competencies.statement: The learner evaluates ethical, legal, and professional standards related to the use of educational technology, including data privacy, intellectual property, accessibility, and digital citizenship, to ensure responsible and compliant instructional practices.

skills.title: Research Design Practices
skills.statement: Researches innovations based on latest design practices, instructional trends, and educational technology.

competencies.title: Selects Educational Technologies through Analysis
competencies.statement: The learner selects educational technologies based on instructional needs across various learning models, including blended and online learning environments, while evaluating emerging technologies for their potential to enhance teaching, learning, and student engagement.

skills.title: Evaluate Curriculum Design Against Requirements
skills.statement: Evaluate curriculum design against learning requirements, audience, and new technologies.

competencies.title: Implements Educational Technology
competencies.statement: The learner implements educational technologies in learning environments using ethical practices and professional standards, ensuring appropriate data use, accessibility, and equitable engagement while monitoring and adjusting practices based on learner needs and outcomes.

skills.title: Evidence-Based Instructional Strategies Implementation
skills.statement: Implement evidence-based instructional strategies with resources and technologies.

courses.name: Educational Research Foundations
courses.scope_notes: This course focuses on research literacy by building foundational knowledge of research principles, methodologies, and processes in education. The course explains key aspects of research, including: qualitative and quantitative approaches, finding resources, research questions, and problem statements. The course also builds out foundations for research analysis, including: data collection methods, reading, interpreting, and analyzing research studies, and data collection instruments. Through these foundational steps, learners are prepared to engage in tasks such as developing annotated bibliographies, conducting literature reviews, and selecting research and data analysis methods.

competencies.title: Analyzes Educational Research Studies
competencies.statement: The learner analyzes educational research studies by interpreting and analyzing data, identifying key findings, and examining the alignment between research questions, methodologies, and conclusions to determine implications for data-informed instructional practices.

skills.title: Public Data Analysis
skills.statement: Analyze data from public databases.

skills.title: Relevant Evidence Collection
skills.statement: Collect relevant evidence to address healthcare questions and problems.

skills.title: Resource Use Demonstration
skills.statement: Demonstrate how to use resources in research.

competencies.title: Interprets Educational Research and Data in Learning Environments
competencies.statement: The learner interprets foundational concepts of educational research and data literacy to read, interpret, and evaluate research findings to inform instructional decisions that improve learning, instruction, curriculum, and training across diverse learning environments.

skills.title: Analyze Data from Research Findings
skills.statement: Analyze data from research findings.

skills.title: Interpret Research Findings
skills.statement: Interpret research findings.

competencies.title: Applies Educational Research Principles
competencies.statement: The learner applies educational research principles by formulating a problem statement and research questions, locating and selecting relevant scholarly sources using academic research tools, and aligning appropriate research methodologies to the identified topic.

skills.title: Research Interdisciplinary Principles
skills.statement: Research interdisciplinary principles from related fields that may improve instructional design practices.

skills.title: Apply Research to Real-World Situations
skills.statement: Apply research to real-world situations.

courses.name: Instructional Leadership
courses.scope_notes: This course focuses on developing core leadership strategies that support effective teaching and learning. The course examines curriculum design and alignment, along with foundational principles of assessment for learning, including how to analyze and use data to guide instructional decisions. The course emphasizes applying common instructional leadership practices to improve curriculum implementation, monitor student progress, and drive data-informed instruction that enhances student outcomes.

competencies.title: Examines the Role of Instructional Leadership
competencies.statement: The learner examines the responsibilities and impact of instructional leadership, including fostering collaborative cultures, supporting educator development, and promoting equitable learning outcomes.

skills.title: Articulate Leadership Characteristics
skills.statement: Articulate the characteristics and components needed to be an effective leader.

skills.title: Collaborative Strategy Development
skills.statement: Develop strategies with organizational team leads.

competencies.title: Designs Instructional Leadership Plans
competencies.statement: The learner designs strategic instructional leadership plans that incorporate evidence-based practices, professional learning structures, and measurable goals to support educator development and student success.

skills.title: Develop Strategic Plans
skills.statement: Develop strategic plans.

skills.title: Implement Evidence-Based Processes for Student Success
skills.statement: Implement evidence-based processes that aid learners in achieving successful outcomes.

competencies.title: Implements Instructional Leadership Practices
competencies.statement: The learner implements instructional leadership strategies that enhance instruction and student achievement through coaching, feedback, and data-driven decision-making.

skills.title: Implement Strategic Plans
skills.statement: Implement strategic plans.

skills.title: Resource Use Decision Making
skills.statement: Make informed decisions to determine the best use of resources for a firm.

skills.title: Strategy Feedback Solicitation
skills.statement: Solicit feedback on strategy, vision, mission, or goals.

courses.name: Diversity and Equity
courses.scope_notes: This course centers on equity, access, and culturally responsive professional practice in literacy development.

competencies.title: Creates Equitable Learning Experiences
competencies.statement: The learner creates equitable learning experiences that promote achievement for all students.

skills.title: Safe and Inclusive Classroom Environment Promotion
skills.statement: Promote a safe and inclusive classroom environment for all students regardless of cultural background or other differences.

competencies.title: Leads Educators in Evaluating Curriculum and Implementing Diverse Instructional Practices
competencies.statement: The learner leads educators in evaluating curriculum and implementing culturally and linguistically diverse instructional practices.

skills.title: Communication Techniques Leveraging
skills.statement: Leverage appropriate communication techniques to persuade and guide outcomes.

skills.title: Help Employees Develop New Skills
skills.statement: Facilitate skill development in employees to enhance performance through coaching.

competencies.title: Advocates for Equitable Systems
competencies.statement: The learner advocates for equitable systems that support multilingual and neurodiverse students within evidence-based standards and frameworks.

skills.title: Develop Equitable and Inclusive Instruction
skills.statement: Develop instruction that demonstrates principles of equitable access and inclusion.

skills.title: Equitable Practices Promotion
skills.statement: Promote equitable practices.

courses.name: Principles and Analysis of Curriculum
courses.display_name: EXXX: Principles and Analysis of Curriculum
courses.scope_notes: This course focuses on evaluating curriculum through the application of established theories, principles, and models, and using that analysis to inform continuous improvement. Learners develop curriculum maps that build on existing skills, prior learning, and historical data, while aligning curriculum to the evolving needs of students through the integration of EdTech and blended teaching models. The course also explores curriculum recommendations that respond to changing standards and demands, ensuring curriculum is accessible to all learners.

competencies.title: Evaluates Curriculum
competencies.statement: The learner evaluates curriculum through the application of theories, principles, and models.

skills.title: Curriculum to Standards Evaluation
skills.statement: Evaluate curriculum against national education or industry standards.

skills.title: Evaluate Curriculum Design Against Requirements
skills.statement: Evaluate curriculum design against learning requirements, audience, and new technologies.

competencies.title: Analyzes Scope and Sequence of a Curriculum Map
competencies.statement: The learner analyzes the scope and sequence of a curriculum map against given standards.

skills.title: Educational Objectives Alignment Evaluation
skills.statement: Evaluate the alignment of educational objectives to the curriculum and instructional goals to meet the learning goals for students.

skills.title: Learning Experiences Sequencing
skills.statement: Sequence learning experiences that align to learning objectives and student goals.

competencies.title: Aligns a Curriculum to Evolving Needs
competencies.statement: The learner aligns a curriculum to the evolving needs of students.

skills.title: Curriculum Alignment
skills.statement: Align curriculum vision, focus, and objectives to the needs of students.

competencies.title: Supports the Implementation of Curriculum
competencies.statement: The learner applies research based principles of implementation science to support stakeholders in implementing curriculum for a diverse group of students.

skills.title: Curriculum Change Selection
skills.statement: Select curriculum areas for change based on data or research.

skills.title: Goal-Aligned Lesson Implementation
skills.statement: Implement single lessons and sequences of lessons that align to curriculum goals and content standards, are relevant to learners, and meet the educational needs of a diverse student population.

courses.name: Instructional Practices and Implementation
courses.scope_notes: This course walks through the evaluation instructional models, theories, and evidence-based approaches that support effective teaching. Learners apply these practices to differentiate instruction, including the integration of EdTech to meet diverse student needs. The course introduces implementation strategies for communicating meaningful feedback, incorporating multi-disciplinary approaches into instruction, and applying evidence-based methods to strengthen student understanding and long-term knowledge retention.

competencies.title: Examines Instructional Models
competencies.statement: The learner examines the application of instructional models against the needs of varied learning environments.

skills.title: Design Model Effectiveness
skills.statement: Determine an effective educational design model to meet the needs of learners.

competencies.title: Differentiates Instruction
competencies.statement: The learner differentiates instruction through the modification of content, process, and product.

skills.title: Students with Exceptionalities Learning Differentiation
skills.statement: Differentiate process, content, products, and the learning environment to meet the needs of individual students with exceptionalities.

competencies.title: Incorporates Evidence-Based Research into Instruction
competencies.statement: The learner incorporates evidence-based research into instruction.

skills.title: Evidence-Based Instructional Strategies Implementation
skills.statement: Implement evidence-based instructional strategies with resources and technologies.

competencies.title: Provides Instructional Feedback
competencies.statement: The learner provides feedback to enhance the implementation of instruction.

skills.title: Peer Feedback Instruction
skills.statement: Instruct learners in peer feedback to improve work.

courses.name: Assessment for Learning
courses.scope_notes: This course focuses on foundational understanding of assessment principles and forms of assessment. It introduces the process for designing and implementing assessments, analyzing assessment validity and risk, and using assessment data. The course explores concepts of assessment enhancement and refinement based on data-driven insights, with analysis and implementation of technology with assessment.

competencies.title: Explains Foundational Principles of Assessment
competencies.statement: The learner explains the foundational principles of assessment, including forms of assessment.

skills.title: Describe Analytic Strategies
skills.statement: Develop assessment strategies using learning analytics.

competencies.title: Analyzes the Validity, Risk, and Reliability of an Assessment
competencies.statement: The learner analyzes the validity, risk, and reliability of an assessment through assessment data analysis.

skills.title: Analyze Evaluation Data
skills.statement: Analyze instructional systems evaluations to improve validity, effectiveness, and efficiency.

skills.title: Instruction and Learning Effectiveness Assessment
skills.statement: Implement ongoing assessment to determine the effectiveness of instruction and student learning.

competencies.title: Analyzes Assessment Data for Instruction
competencies.statement: The learner analyzes data to adjust instruction, enhance student achievement, and inform feedback.

skills.title: Achievement Measurement Assessments Development
skills.statement: Develop assessments to measure student achievement of the learning outcomes.

skills.title: Apply Assessment Methods
skills.statement: Apply assessment strategies and methods that align to instructional goals and learning outcomes.

competencies.title: Designs an Assessment System
competencies.statement: The learner designs a balanced and comprehensive assessment system that aligns to standards and measures progress toward learning goals.

skills.title: Learner Assessment Strategy Development
skills.statement: Develop a learner assessment strategy.

courses.name: MSCIN Capstone
courses.scope_notes: The Master of Science in Curriculum and Instruction Capstone is the culminating course of the degree. It unites content area knowledge with the completion of a research project or study.

competencies.title: Preparing to do Research
competencies.statement: The learner produces a research proposal that satisfies and cites specific laws, policies, codes of ethics, professional standards, and other applicable requirements to advance student learning.

competencies.title: Capstone Literature Review and Methodology
competencies.statement: The learner writes a scholarly literature review and research methodology chapters that align with the approved research proposal.

competencies.title: Conducts the Research Process
competencies.statement: The learner conducts their research project and collects data consistent with their proposal.

competencies.title: Results and Conclusions
competencies.statement: The learner writes the results and conclusions chapters to align with the approved research proposal after collecting and analyzing the data for the research project or study.

competencies.title: Presenting the Capstone
competencies.statement: The learner presents a completed capstone supported by a narrated multimedia presentation that could potentially contribute to knowledge in the field of curriculum and instruction.
